skill.name: running-claude-code-via-litellm-copilot
skill.description: Use when routing Claude Code through a local LiteLLM proxy to GitHub Copilot, reducing direct Anthropic spend, configuring ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL or ANTHROPIC_MODEL overrides, or troubleshooting Copilot proxy setup failures.
skill.summary: Route Claude Code requests through a local LiteLLM proxy to GitHub Copilot for cost optimization while maintaining the Claude Code workflow.
skill.author: xixu-me

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 99 patterns but all are false positives. The scanned files are markdown documentation containing code examples and instructions, not executable code. Backticks detected are markdown formatting for code blocks. References to shell commands, PowerShell, URLs, and file paths are instructional content for users to execute manually. No actual cryptographic operations, network calls, or filesystem access code exists. The skill is documentation-only guidance for setting up a LiteLLM proxy between Claude Code and GitHub Copilot.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Command Examples

low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Commands are clearly presented as documentation examples with explanatory context. No obfuscation or hidden execution paths detected.

content.user_title: Route Claude Code Through LiteLLM Proxy to GitHub Copilot
content.value_statement: Reduce Anthropic API costs by routing Claude Code requests through a local LiteLLM proxy to GitHub Copilot. This skill provides step-by-step setup instructions for configuring the proxy workaround.
actual_capabilities: Provides instructions for setting up LiteLLM as a local proxy between Claude Code and GitHub Copilot
Explains how to configure ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL and ANTHROPIC_MODEL environment variables
Guides users through GitHub Copilot device authorization flow with LiteLLM
Provides troubleshooting steps for common proxy setup failures like model-not-found and 401/403 errors
Explains both temporary session-based and persistent configuration approaches
limitations: This is a workaround not officially supported or endorsed by GitHub
Requires a local LiteLLM proxy server to be running continuously while using Claude Code
Model availability depends on what GitHub Copilot exposes and may change without notice
Does not provide legal or terms-of-service guidance regarding GitHub Copilot usage

use_cases.title: Cost-Conscious Developer
use_cases.description: A developer wants to reduce direct Anthropic API spending while maintaining their Claude Code workflow by leveraging their existing GitHub Copilot subscription.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Troubleshooting
use_cases.description: A DevOps engineer needs to debug why Claude Code requests are not reaching the LiteLLM proxy or are returning authentication errors from GitHub Copilot.

use_cases.title: Technical Explorer
use_cases.description: A technically sophisticated user wants to understand the proxy architecture and set up a flexible local development environment with multiple AI backend options.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Setup Question
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to set up Claude Code to work with GitHub Copilot through LiteLLM. What are the basic steps to get started?
prompt_templates.scenario: User is new to this workaround and needs a high-level overview before diving into implementation details.

prompt_templates.title: Configuration Help
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a LiteLLM config.yaml file for GitHub Copilot with the claude-opus-4.5 model.
prompt_templates.scenario: User understands the concept and needs specific configuration file content for their setup.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshooting Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Claude Code starts but LiteLLM shows no incoming requests. I set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to http://localhost:4000. What should I check?
prompt_templates.scenario: User has completed initial setup but the request chain is broken somewhere between Claude Code and LiteLLM.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Header Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: My requests reach GitHub Copilot but get rejected. Do I need to add custom headers like editor-version or user-agent to my LiteLLM config?
prompt_templates.scenario: User has the basic setup working but needs advanced configuration to handle specific rejection patterns from the Copilot API.

output_examples.input: I need help creating the LiteLLM config.yaml file
output_examples.output: Here is a minimal config.yaml for GitHub Copilot with claude-opus-4.5. Place this in your working directory before starting LiteLLM. The model_name field defines what Claude Code will request. The model field uses the github_copilot provider prefix. The drop_params setting removes Anthropic-specific fields that Copilot does not understand.

output_examples.input: How do I verify my setup is working correctly?
output_examples.output: Use two terminals: keep LiteLLM running in Terminal A, then start claude in Terminal B. Send a small request like a simple code explanation. Watch Terminal A for incoming request logs showing the github_copilot route. If you see the request flow through and get a response, your setup is working.

best_practices: Start with temporary environment variables for testing before making persistent changes to settings.json
Keep ANTHROPIC_MODEL value identical to the model_name in your LiteLLM config.yaml for proper routing
Keep the LiteLLM terminal visible during initial setup since logs provide the fastest debugging feedback
anti_patterns: Do not replace your entire ~/.claude/settings.json file - merge only the env keys this workflow requires
Do not treat ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN as a real Copilot credential - it is a local placeholder that Claude Code expects
Do not add custom header overrides as your first attempt - use the basic provider flow and only add headers if specific errors indicate they are needed

faq.question: Is this workflow officially supported by GitHub?
faq.answer: No. This is a community-discovered workaround using LiteLLM as a local proxy. You should evaluate GitHub Copilot terms of service and usage limits yourself before using this approach.

faq.question: Do I need a real Anthropic API key for ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN?
faq.answer: No. ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN is a local placeholder because Claude Code expects a non-empty value. LiteLLM handles the actual authentication with GitHub Copilot through a separate device authorization flow.

faq.question: What happens if LiteLLM stops running?
faq.answer: Claude Code will fail to connect because ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL points to your local LiteLLM instance. You must keep LiteLLM running on the configured port while using Claude Code with this setup.

faq.question: Why do I need device authorization with GitHub Copilot?
faq.answer: LiteLLM authenticates with GitHub Copilot on your behalf. The first request triggers GitHub's device authorization flow where you approve access through a browser. LiteLLM then stores the credential locally for subsequent requests.

faq.question: Can I use any Claude model through this setup?
faq.answer: You can use whatever models GitHub Copilot exposes. The model name in ANTHROPIC_MODEL must exactly match the model_name in your LiteLLM config. Copilot's available models may change over time.

faq.question: What if I get model-not-found or 404 errors?
faq.answer: First verify that ANTHROPIC_MODEL exactly matches the model_name in config.yaml including case and hyphens. Then confirm LiteLLM is running and receiving requests. Finally check that your GitHub Copilot account has access to the requested model.
